lesson.menu_desc: Status Updates, Present Perfect and Linking Words -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 7 -- Reporting to the Director -- Present Perfect and Linking Words
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Walyson Ginaldo Silva | Aula 7 -- Reporting to the Director

listenings.text: Hi, this is Walyson with a quick status update on the Europe project. So far, we have made very good progress. We have already signed two contracts with new buyers, and we have reached an important milestone: we have achieved our target volume for the quarter. In addition, the logistics team has improved the shipping plan, so the project is on track. However, I want to highlight one problem. The samples from one origin have not arrived yet, therefore there has been a small delay on one order. The status of that order is still pending, but I have contacted the supplier, and as a result they have promised the samples for Monday. I have not finished the final report yet, but I will send a full overview to the director by Friday. Overall, the quarter has been strong, and I am confident we will hit every target.

Hello Walyson, this is Diana, the director. Thank you for your update. I have read your report, and I am very pleased with the progress. The team has done excellent work this quarter. We have signed more contracts than last year, and we have already passed our main milestone. However, I have one question about the delay. I understand the samples have not arrived yet, so please keep me informed about that pending order. In addition, the board has asked for a short overview of our results, therefore I would like you to prepare a few slides for next week. Highlight the contracts we have won and the targets we have achieved. As a result of your strong work, the board is happy, and we are on track for a great year. Well done, and let me know if you need any help with the presentation.

extra_audio.text: First, give a short overview of the project so far. Next, highlight the milestones you have reached and the targets you have achieved. Then, report any problem, for example a delay or a pending order. After that, explain what you have done about it and the result. Finally, say what is still on track and when you will send the full report.
